NetName: SPECIAL-IPV4-LOOPBACK-IANA-RESERVED
NetHandle: NET-127-0-0-0-1
Parent: ()
NetType: IANA Special Use
OriginAS: 
Organization: Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA)
RegDate: 
Updated: 2013-08-30
Comment: Addresses starting with "127." are used when one program needs to talk to another program running on the same machine using the Internet 
Comment: Protocol. 127.0.0.1 is the most commonly used address and is called the "loopback" address.
Comment: 
Comment: These addresses were assigned by the IETF, the organization that develops Internet protocols, in the Standard document, RFC 1122, which can 
Comment: be found here:
Comment: http://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc1122

crowdsourced_context.details: This IP was involved in 154391 events across 27 distinct attack types. Attacks: rdp-proxy (124183), ssh-process (18871), postgresql-query (3385), tcp-portscan (2527), postgresql-login (1018), mysql-query (1014), smb3-login (828), mysql-login (648), mssql-login (601), mssql-query (282), rdp-event (183), ssh-login (178), mysql-info (131), smb-login (131), mysql-db (83), mysql-command (77), mysql-connection (69), mysql-client-detect (44), mysql-handshake-debug (42), mysql-routing (42), ftp-login (17), mysql-error (17), SIS_odtu-login (11), ssh-command (3), mysql-greeting (2), postgresql-meta (2), ssh-cisco-login (2). NetHandle: NET-224-0-0-0-1
Parent: ()
NetType: IANA Special Use
OriginAS: 
Organization: Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA)
RegDate: 1991-05-22
Updated: 2024-05-24
Comment: Addresses starting with a number between 224 and 239 are used for IP multicast. IP multicast is a technology for efficiently sending the same content to multiple destinations. It is commonly used for distributing financial information and video streams, among other things.
Comment: 
Comment: A full list of IPv4 multicast assignments can be found at:
Comment: 
Comment: http://www.iana.org/assignments/multicast-addresses
Comment: 
Comment: A document describing the policies for assigning multicast addresses can be found at:
Comment: http://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc5771
